'''Brett Kearney''' (born 29 September 1983 in [[Sydney]], New South Wales), also known by his  initials '''"BK"''', is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer formerly with the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]], now currently playing for the [[Collegians]] in the [[Illawarra Rugby League]] competition. A utility [[Utility back|back]], he has represented Country Origin and previously played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla]].

==Biography==

===Early career===
Kearney's ''Junior Club'' was the Kincumber Colts, on the [[Central Coast, New South Wales|Central Coast]]. Kearney was recruited to play with the [[North Sydney Bears]] in the [[Harold Matthews Cup]] and [[S.G. Ball Cup]] before he moved on to [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] in 2003.

===2003-2005===
Kearney made his ''First Grade Debut'' in round 1 of the [[2003 NRL season]] for South Sydney against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] at [[Telstra Stadium]].  Kearney made a total of 28 appearances for South Sydney as the club struggled towards the bottom of the table, finishing last in 2003 and 2004.

===2006-2014===
Signing with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], Kearney had limited opportunity in 2006, with the exception of injuries. In 2007, with a positional change to fullback, Kearney was a regular in the first grade side until he suffered injury during the season. At fullback, "BK" made inroads in attack, scoring several tries. He scored a superb hat trick in the 2007 game against [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], showing his ability in attack.
In 2008, Kearney was selected to play in the annual City vs Country match.  At club level, Kearney made 20 appearances for Cronulla in the [[2008 NRL season]] and scored 10 tries as the club reached the preliminary final but were defeated 28-0 by [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].

In the [[2009 NRL season]], Kearney suffered a season-ending injury in round 1.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/aug/03/leigh-centurions-ian-millward-robbie-paul/|title=Robbie Paul joins Ian Millward at Leigh Centurions|website=The Guardian|date=3 August 2009}}</ref>

In 2010, he joined Super League club [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]]. After featuring for the at stand-off at the start of his Bulls career he was shifted to his natural position of full back midway through the 2010 season. After [[Mick Potter]] took over, Kearney featured almost exclusively as a full back in 2011.

In 2011, Kearney signed a new three-year deal with Bradford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/9106826.double-delight-as-brett-kearney-toasts-victory-and-new-bradford-bulls-contract/|title=Double delight as Brett Kearney toasts victory and new Bradford Bulls contract|website=The Telegraph and argus}}</ref>

===Return to Australia===
In 2015, following five seasons in the Super League, Kearney returned to Australia after being linked to Illawarra Coal League team, the Collegians.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?client=0-2373-0-0-0&sID=19818&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=33475065 |title=Former Rabbitoh and Shark Brett Kearney signs up with Collegians - Illawarra District Rugby League - FOX SPORTS PULSE |access-date=20 August 2015 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304075348/http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?client=0-2373-0-0-0&sID=19818&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=33475065 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

In 2018, Kearney returned to his junior club, the Kincumber Colts.

In 2023, the Brain Foundation announced the appointment of Kearney as an ambassador.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://brainfoundation.org.au/brett-kearney/ |title=Brett Kearney - Concussion in Sport - Brain Foundation |access-date=25 March 2025}}</ref>
